:::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} ## Parole Eligibility
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

How is Parole Release Eligibility Determined?

Only felons are eligible for parole in Nevada because they are the only offenders sent to prison. A person sentenced for a crime committed on or after July 1, 1995 may be paroled when they have served the minimum term.8 Each felony sentence has a minimum and maximum term unless a definite term is required by statute. If an individual is sentenced to consecutive terms of imprisonment, the court must aggregate the minimum terms of imprisonment to determine release eligibility. If an individual is sentenced to concurrent terms of imprisonment, eligibility for parole occurs after the longest of the minimum terms has been served. In some cases, good time may be deducted from the minimum term and hasten parole eligibility.


People who were Eligible for Parole in 2020

In 2020, White, non-Hispanic people constituted the most number of people eligible for parole but still in prison, accounting for 39% (933 people) of the parole-eligible prison population.


In 2020, violent offenses constituted the most serious sentenced offense for individuals eligible for parole but still in prison, accounting for 47% (1,135 people) of the parole-eligible prison population.


``` ::: :::

## Prison Population
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Prison Population

In 2020, there were 2,395 people who were eligible for parole but not released from prison, constituting 22% of the parole-eligible prison population.




``` ::: :::

## Length of Stay
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
